content.full_text:
County of Sacramento
Office of the County Counsel
700 H Street, Suite 2650    Sacramento, CA 95814    Telephone (916) 440-5544    FAX (916) 440-8207

COUNTY COUNSEL
L. B. Elam

ASSISTANT COUNTY
COUNSEL
Robert L. Pleines
Melvyn W. Price

SUPERVISING DEPUTY
COUNTY COUNSEL
Clement J. Dougherty, Jr.
Monte L. Fuller
Barry Steiner
Kathleen A. O'Connor
Michele Bach
John Whisenhunt
Robert A. Ryan, Jr.

DEPUTY COUNTY
COUNSEL
J. Steven Burris
Elaine P. DiPietro
Lilly C. Frawley
Kathryn A. Shurtleff
Anthony L. Wright
Richard G. Llata
John H. Dodds
Denis J. Zialf
Ramona A. Armistead
Steven M. Basha
Renaldo Carboni
James G. Wight
Steven Kaiser
Peggy W. Sylvester
Martha E. Potriades
Latoya Montgomery
Steven P. Rudolph
John T. Seyman
Catherine Schmid
Patrick I. McCreal
Ray C. Thompson
Timothy D. Weiland

[illegible] 1992

June 26, 1992

General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA    95804-0807

Re:        Request for Conflict of Interest Opinion

Dear Sir:

We hereby request, on behalf of Sacramento County
Supervisor Sandra Smoley, a written opinion advising
whether she is required to disqualify herself from
participating in a matter to be heard by the Board of
Supervisors on August 19, 1992.

The conflict of interest issue arises from a recent
proposal by Mercy Healthcare Sacramento, to establish a
trauma center at its Mercy-American River Hospital. The
proposal of Mercy Healthcare Sacramento was made in
response to a Request For Proposals issued by the County
of Sacramento. Currently, the proposal is under review
and evaluation by County staff. On August 19, 1992, the
proposal will be presented to the Board of Supervisors of
the County for a determination of whether or not the
County should enter into a contract, and provide funding,
for such trauma center services by Mercy Healthcare
Sacramento.

Currently, the University of California at Davis is
the only provider of trauma center services in the County
of Sacramento. Said services are provided pursuant to a
contract with the County. Any contract with Mercy
Healthcare Sacramento for trauma center services would be
supplemental to contract services provided by the
University of California. The University currently
admits approximately 4,000 trauma patients per year. If
the County enters into the contract with Mercy
Healthcare, approximately 2,000 of those 4,000 annual
trauma patients would be diverted to Mercy Healthcare.

Fair Political Practices                2              June 26, 1992
Commission


     The University of California has taken a public position
supporting the development of a second trauma center at Mercy-
American River Hospital.  This office has been advised by the
staff at the County Health Department that certain consequences
result where the University is the sole provider of trauma center
services in the County.  The University has expressed concern
with overcrowding resulting from a perceived excess of trauma
patients at its hospital.  Moreover, the extremely high volume of
trauma patients in relation to patients undergoing elective
surgery has educational and financial repercussions.  Surgical
training at the University hospital involves an undue emphasis on
trauma injuries and a correspondingly insufficient opportunity
for other kinds of surgical training.  Accordingly, the
University hospital is less satisfactory as a teaching facility.
In addition, there is historical evidence that, as opposed to
patients undergoing elective surgery, trauma patients are less
able to pay for their medical care.

     Ms. Smoley has no financial interest in Mercy Healthcare
Sacramento.  However, by virtue of her husband's interests in an
architectural firm, she does have an existing financial interest
in the University of California.  That is, within the past 12
months her husband, or his firm, has received payment for
architectural services from the University in sufficient amounts
to constitute a financial interest within the meaning of the Act.

     By your advice letter No. I-89-467, dated May 4, 1990, you
described the circumstances wherein Ms. Smoley is prohibited from
participating in decisions which affect clients of her husband's
architectural firm.  More specifically, that letter advised that
she may not participate in any decision she knows or has reason
to know will have a reasonably foreseeable financial effect,
distinguishable from the effect on the public generally, on any
client of her husband's architectural firm who has provided
income to such firm of $2,174 or more in the 12 months prior to
the time when the decision is made.

     Based upon your letter, we have advised that until 12 months
following the receipt by Ms. Smoley's husband, personally, of
$500 or by his architectural firm of $2,174, for services to the
University of California, Ms. Smoley must disqualify herself from
any decision of the Board of Supervisors in which the University,
though not a subject of the proceeding, will incur an increase or
decrease in gross revenues, assets, or liabilities in the amount
of $1 million or more, or an increase or decrease in expenditures
of $250,000 or more.  (2 Cal. Admin. Code 18702.5 (a)).

Fair Political Practices                3              June 26, 1992
Commission


     Based upon the above-described facts, we have concluded that
the University would not be the subject of the proceeding to
determine whether a second trauma center should be designed at
Mercy-American River Hospital. However, we have advised that it
is reasonably foreseeable that such a decision, made in the face
of express support by the University, could have a substantial
financial effect on the University.

     We do not have specific facts which would indicate that the
expenditures will result in an increase or decrease of the
University's gross revenues, assets or liabilities in the amount
of $1 million or more, or will increase or decrease in the
University's expenditures by $250,000 or more. We have, however,
advised Ms. Smoley that she should disqualify herself from
participating in any such proceedings regarding the designation
of the second trauma facility at Mercy-American River Hospital
unless she can obtain from the University of California written
verification that the designation of a second trauma center would
not affect the University's expenditures on revenues in amounts
in excess of $250,000 or $1 million respectively.

     Ms. Smoley desires a further opinion from the Fair Political
Practices Commission and has a need for a response prior to the
August 19, 1992 hearing.

     If I can be of further assistance, please contact me at
(916) 440-5558.

                                        Very truly yours,

                                        L. B. ELAM, County Counsel

                                        By ______________________
                                           MELVYN W. PRICE
                                           Assistant County Counsel

cc: Sandra Smoley


MWP:pdf
fppc.ltr

sections.question_synthetic: Whether Sacramento County Supervisor Sandra Smoley must disqualify herself from participating in a Board of Supervisors decision regarding a proposed trauma center contract with Mercy Healthcare Sacramento, given her husband's financial interests in the University of California through his architectural firm.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from Sacramento County Counsel to FPPC, not an FPPC response. No QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections are present in standard format. The document details a conflict of interest situation and references a prior FPPC advice letter (I-89-467) but does not contain FPPC's response to this request. Requestor indicates need for response prior to August 19, 1992 hearing.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: Whether Sacramento County Supervisor Sandra Smoley must disqualify herself from participating in a Board of Supervisors decision regarding a proposed trauma center contract with Mercy Healthcare Sacramento, given her husband's financial interests in the University of California through his architectural firm.

embedding.summary: County Counsel requests an FPPC opinion on whether Supervisor Sandra Smoley should disqualify herself from a Board decision on a trauma center contract proposal, due to her husband's architectural firm having received payments from UC Davis, which has a financial interest in the outcome.
